chapter_goal: Use flows, tubular neighborhoods, and the homotopy formula as concrete local tools for later Moser normal-form arguments.

library_routing.why: a local flow is best inspected as a deformed grid with arrows
normal fibers and projection failure are planar and need labels, not 3D rotation
the lecture is a chain of proof tools feeding the Moser trick
the residual can be checked exactly in a toy normal-bundle model

visual_sequence.inspection_target: arrows generate the visible grid motion
small normal bands have unique projection; the center failure has many nearest points
Cartan formula plus pullback differentiation feed the homotopy formula
